chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا أُعْطِيَ الْأَئِمَّةُ ( عليهم السلام ) مِنِ اسْمِ اللَّهِ الْأَعْظَمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 36 – What has been Given to the Imams<sup>asws</sup> from the Magnificent Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/37)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/37)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ
أَبِي الْحَسَنِ صَاحِبِ الْعَسْكَرِ ( عليه السلام )

text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ اسْمُ اللَّهِ الْأَعْظَمُ ثَلَاثَةٌ وَ سَبْعُونَ حَرْفاً كَانَ عِنْدَ آصَفَ حَرْفٌ فَتَكَلَّمَ بِهِ فَانْخَرَقَتْ لَهُ الْأَرْضُ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ سَبَإٍ فَتَنَاوَلَ عَرْشَ بِلْقِيسَ حَتَّى صَيَّرَهُ إِلَى سُلَيْمَانَ ثُمَّ انْبَسَطَتِ الْأَرْضُ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ طَرْفَةِ عَيْنٍ وَ عِنْدَنَا مِنْهُ اثْنَانِ وَ سَبْعُونَ حَرْفاً وَ حَرْفٌ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ مُسْتَأْثِرٌ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad Al Ashary, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Ali Bin Muhammad Al Nowfaly,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, the owner of the army, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The Magnificent Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> are seventy three letters. One letter was with Asif (Bin Barkhiyya<sup>as</sup>, successor<sup>as</sup> of Suleyman<sup>as</sup>). So he<sup>as</sup> spoke with it, and the land was folded for him, in what was between him<sup>as</sup> and Saba (Sheba). So he<sup>as</sup> grabbed the throne of Bilquis until it came to be to Suleyman<sup>as</sup>. Then the earth extended back in less than the blink of an eye. And with us<sup>asws</sup>, from it, are seventy-two letters, and one letter is in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Accounting by it in the Knowledge of the unseen’.
en.sarwar: 3. al-Husayn ibn Muhammad al-Ash‘ari from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abdallah from Ali ibn Muhammad al-Nawfali who has said that he heard abu al-Hassan (a.s.) Sahib al-‘Askar say the following. “The greatest name of Allah has seventy three letters. There was only one letter with Asif. He spoke with it and the land between him and the throne of the Queen of Sheba (Bilqis) sunk down as such that he took her throne and placed it before Solomon. The land then came to normal state within less than a blinking of an eye. There are seventy-two of those letters with us. One letter is with Allah which, He has kept it exclusively in the knowledge of the unseen.”
